{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.0,
    "R1.2": 0.0,
    "R1.3": 0.0,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.3,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "When you say “Clean this up,” which scope do you prefer? A) Copyedit only: sentence/word-level clarity, grammar, concision, and tone; no reordering or content changes (R1.6). B) Copyedit plus light restructuring: adjust sentence/paragraph order and gently temper claims for clarity/consistency; no new content (R1.6 + limited R1.5). C) Deeper rewrite: reframe the thesis/claims and check argument/evidence alignment, potentially changing content emphasis (R1.1/R1.2/R1.3 + R1.6).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "“Clean this up” most directly implies sentence-level polish for clarity, grammar, and concision without altering core content.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should preserve and, if needed, clarify stated uncertainties and limitations already present in the text.",
    "RX.2": "Language should avoid inadvertently strengthening claims beyond what the described results support.",
    "RX.3": "Work must remain within the requested cleanup scope and avoid expanding into new analysis or content changes unless approved.",
    "RX.4": "Wording should not introduce phrasing that could mislead stakeholders about deployment readiness or safety.",
    "RX.5": "Revisions should maintain traceability to the provided text and keep changes transparent and attributable to the original artifact."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: When you say “Clean this up,” which scope do you prefer? A) Copyedit only: sentence/word-level clarity, grammar, concision, and tone; no reordering or content changes (R1.6). B) Copyedit plus light restructuring: adjust sentence/paragraph order and gently temper claims for clarity/consistency; no new content (R1.6 + limited R1.5). C) Deeper rewrite: reframe the thesis/claims and check argument/evidence alignment, potentially changing content emphasis (R1.1/R1.2/R1.3 + R1.6).

rationale.R1.6: “Clean this up” most directly implies sentence-level polish for clarity, grammar, and concision without altering core content.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should preserve and, if needed, clarify stated uncertainties and limitations already present in the text.
rationale.RX.2: Language should avoid inadvertently strengthening claims beyond what the described results support.
rationale.RX.3: Work must remain within the requested cleanup scope and avoid expanding into new analysis or content changes unless approved.
rationale.RX.4: Wording should not introduce phrasing that could mislead stakeholders about deployment readiness or safety.
rationale.RX.5: Revisions should maintain traceability to the provided text and keep changes transparent and attributable to the original artifact.
